title: Journalism Ethics
description: Verification, attribution, conflict of interest, off-the-record, correction standards.
summary_pt_br: Ética jornalística: verificar, atribuir, conflito de interesse, off the record, correções.

scenes.subtitle_en: Verification, attribution, conflict of interest, off-the-record, correction standards.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Ética jornalística: verificar, atribuir, conflito de interesse, off the record, correções.

scenes.text_en: Verification, attribution, conflict of interest, off-the-record, correction standards. Focus: journalism ethics. Every claim should be independently verified before publication.
scenes.text_pt: Ética jornalística: verificar, atribuir, conflito de interesse, off the record, correções. Foco: journalism ethics. Toda afirmação deve ser verificada independentemente.

scenes.title: Journalism Ethics
scenes.body_en: Key principles: VERIFY before publish. ATTRIBUTE every claim. Disclose CONFLICTS OF INTEREST. Distinguish OFF-THE-RECORD from ON-THE-RECORD. Issue CORRECTIONS promptly.
scenes.body_pt: Ética jornalística: verificar, atribuir, conflito de interesse, off the record, correções.

examples.en: Every claim should be independently verified before publication.
examples.pt: Toda afirmação deve ser verificada independentemente.

examples.en: Anonymous sources require at least two corroborators.
examples.pt: Fontes anônimas exigem pelo menos dois corroboradores.

examples.en: Corrections should be as visible as the original error.
examples.pt: Correções devem ser tão visíveis quanto o erro original.

exercise.question: Complete: "Every claim should be ___ verified before publication."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Every claim should be independently verified before publication."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Toda afirmação deve ser verificada independentemente.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Anonymous sources require at least two corroborators.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Anonymous sources require at least two corroborators."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Fontes anônimas exigem pelo menos dois corroboradores.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Journalism Ethics — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre journalism ethics. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You have a scoop but only one source. What now?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: The source wants to stay anonymous.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Document why, and verify with a second source.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You made a factual error last week.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Issue a correction as prominent as the error.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: A company you once worked for is involved.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Disclose it or hand off the story.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: An editor asks you to soften a quote.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Ethics is journalism's only real competitive advantage.

scenes.title: The newsroom standards meeting
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize the five principles discussed. Use at least three journalism terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Every Thursday morning, the newsroom gathered to review the principles that had been tested that week.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Every factual claim should be independently verified before the story leaves this building, no exceptions.

paragraphs.speaker: Editor
paragraphs.en: Anonymous sources may be necessary, but they require at least two independent corroborators to appear in print.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: A correction, when warranted, must be as prominent and visible as the original reporting error itself.

paragraphs.speaker: Editor
paragraphs.en: Conflicts of interest — including prior employment — must be disclosed before the story is assigned.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Ethics, Kumar told young reporters, was the single quality no algorithm could ever replace in journalism.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Journalism Ethics". Verification, attribution, conflict of interest, off-the-record, correction standards. Every claim should be independently verified before publication. Anonymous sources require at least two corroborators. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Journalism Ethics". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
